skill.name: hybrid-cloud-networking
skill.description: Configure secure, high-performance connectivity between on-premises infrastructure and cloud platforms using VPN and dedicated connections. Use when building hybrid cloud architectures, connecting data centers to cloud, or implementing secure cross-premises networking.
skill.summary: Plan hybrid cloud network connectivity with VPN, dedicated links, routing, monitoring, and failover guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are Markdown examples, topology diagrams, and cloud networking terms. No malicious intent, prompt injection, data exfiltration, or executable skill code was found. The remaining risk is low because the skill includes cloud CLI examples that should only be run in authorized environments.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The reported backtick execution findings are Markdown code fences, inline reference paths, diagrams, and example infrastructure snippets. They are not Ruby backtick execution and do not execute automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown documentation and the flagged regions are fenced blocks or inline paths. No executable Ruby, shell substitution, script file, or automation hook is present.

low_findings.title: Documentation IP Addresses Are Example Network Values
low_findings.description: The hardcoded IP findings are example network addresses used in infrastructure documentation. The public address uses the documentation range 203.0.113.0/24, and the private CIDR is a common routing example.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The addresses appear in Terraform and BGP examples, not in outbound network code. There is no evidence of callback infrastructure or data transmission.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance And C2 Keyword Findings Are Domain Vocabulary
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged networking terms, VPN commands, telemetry, routing, and monitoring language as reconnaissance or C2 indicators. In context, these terms describe legitimate hybrid cloud network design and troubleshooting.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged words are expected in a hybrid networking skill and are tied to benign architecture and troubleshooting guidance. No evidence found of command-and-control behavior or unauthorized reconnaissance.

dangerous_patterns.title: Cloud CLI Examples Require Authorization
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes AWS and Azure CLI troubleshooting commands. They are read-only examples, but users should run them only in accounts and subscriptions they are authorized to inspect.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible CLI examples and could access cloud account metadata if copied into a configured shell. They are not automatically executed by the skill and do not show malicious intent.

content.value_statement: Hybrid cloud projects need reliable connectivity, routing, security, and failover across data centers and cloud platforms. This skill guides VPN, Direct Connect, ExpressRoute, Interconnect, BGP, monitoring, and cost decisions.
actual_capabilities: Compares VPN, AWS Direct Connect, Azure ExpressRoute, and Google Cloud Interconnect options.
Describes hub-and-spoke, multi-region, and multi-cloud hybrid network patterns.
Provides Terraform-style examples for AWS and Azure VPN gateways.
Explains BGP routing, route propagation, route filtering, and advertised CIDR planning.
Lists monitoring metrics, troubleshooting commands, cost controls, and high availability practices.
limitations: Does not include complete production Terraform modules.
Does not validate live cloud accounts or network devices automatically.
References additional files that are not present in the scanned package.
Requires provider-specific review before implementation in regulated environments.

use_cases.title: Plan a data center to cloud connection
use_cases.description: Compare VPN and dedicated connectivity options before extending on-premises infrastructure to a cloud VPC or VNet.

use_cases.title: Design resilient hybrid routing
use_cases.description: Use BGP, route propagation, dual tunnels, and active-active patterns to reduce outage impact.

use_cases.title: Review hybrid network operations
use_cases.description: Check monitoring metrics, troubleshooting commands, security controls, and cost optimization choices.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a connection option
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose between site-to-site VPN and dedicated connectivity for a hybrid cloud network. My bandwidth, latency, budget, and availability requirements are: [details].

prompt_templates.title: Draft a hub-and-spoke design
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a hub-and-spoke hybrid network design for [cloud provider] with on-premises connectivity, production, staging, and development networks.

prompt_templates.title: Review BGP routing
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this hybrid cloud BGP routing plan for route propagation, filtering, failover, and CIDR risks: [routing plan].

prompt_templates.title: Build an operations checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an operations checklist for a multi-cloud hybrid network using VPN, dedicated links, flow logs, tunnel telemetry, and cost controls.

output_examples.input: I need to connect a data center to AWS with moderate bandwidth and limited budget.
output_examples.output: A practical recommendation comparing site-to-site VPN and Direct Connect, with tradeoffs for cost, latency, setup time, redundancy, and monitoring.

output_examples.input: Review my hybrid routing plan for two regions and one on-premises data center.
output_examples.output: A structured review covering BGP advertisements, failover behavior, route filtering, overlapping CIDR risks, and monitoring metrics.

output_examples.input: Prepare a security checklist for Azure ExpressRoute and VPN backup.
output_examples.output: A checklist covering private connectivity, encryption, network ACLs, flow logs, DDoS protection, endpoint design, and regular audits.

best_practices: Confirm bandwidth, latency, availability, compliance, and budget before selecting a connectivity model.
Use redundant tunnels or links with BGP failover for production hybrid networks.
Document route advertisements, filtering rules, monitoring metrics, and ownership before deployment.
anti_patterns: Using one VPN tunnel as the only path for critical production traffic.
Advertising broad CIDR ranges without route filtering or overlap checks.
Running cloud troubleshooting commands without confirming account authorization and scope.

faq.question: Does this skill deploy network infrastructure?
faq.answer: No. It provides planning guidance and examples. Deployment requires separate implementation and review.

faq.question: Which cloud providers are covered?
faq.answer: It covers AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud hybrid connectivity patterns.

faq.question: Can it help choose VPN or dedicated connectivity?
faq.answer: Yes. It compares cost, bandwidth, latency, reliability, and setup tradeoffs.

faq.question: Does it include routing guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers BGP, route propagation, filtering, advertisements, and failover considerations.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found documentation examples, not malicious code. Users should authorize any cloud CLI usage.

faq.question: What should users prepare before using it?
faq.answer: Prepare cloud provider details, CIDR ranges, bandwidth needs, availability goals, security requirements, and budget constraints.
